title: Speaking – Part 2: Long Turn
description: Each candidate compares two of three photographs and answers two questions about them. You have about one minute. Then your partner answers a brief question.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the photographs and say what challenge the people might be facing and how they might be feeling.
tasks.mainQuestion: What challenge might the people be facing, and how might they be feeling?
tasks.followUpQuestion: Which situation would you find most challenging?

images.label: a
images.description: A young woman in hiking gear climbs a steep rocky trail in a dense forest, looking determined as she grips onto protruding tree roots.

images.label: b
images.description: An elderly man sits in front of a computer screen in a dimly lit room, squinting as he adjusts his glasses and takes notes beside a stack of papers.

images.label: c
images.description: A group of teenagers is caught in a rainstorm while trying to set up a tent in an open field, their expressions a mix of frustration and laughter.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the photographs and say what decision the people might be making and what factors they might be considering.
tasks.mainQuestion: What decision might the people be making, and what factors might they be considering?
tasks.followUpQuestion: Which decision would be hardest for you to make?

images.label: d
images.description: A middle-aged couple standing at a crossroads in a bustling city, looking at a map with puzzled expressions, deciding which way to go.

images.label: e
images.description: A student sits surrounded by textbooks in a university library, pondering over course enrollment forms, appearing deep in thought.

images.label: f
images.description: A chef in a busy kitchen examines two different sets of plated dishes, seeming to weigh options with a discerning eye.
